# Postman/Newman Sandbox Corpus

These fixtures are exported Postman Collection v2.1 JSON documents used as the sandbox v1 compatibility corpus. The upstream Newman reference target is the current npm `latest` dist-tag resolved on April 25, 2026: `newman@6.2.2`.

- `newman-sandbox-v1.collection.json` covers async ordering, timers/microtasks, nested `pm.sendRequest`, iteration data, current-URL and jar-style cookie helpers including hostname and object-set forms, variable precedence, variable metadata/clear/toJSON behavior, documented dynamic variables, Postman globals, bundled `pm.require`/`require` packages including the common Postman built-in facade set and Postman-listed NodeJS module facades, hardened Postman Collection SDK request/response/list object facades, reviewed cached team/external package imports, Chai-compatible assertions, `pm.test.skip`/`pm.test.index`/skipped result shape, `pm.execution.location`, `pm.execution.runRequest`, safe `pm.visualizer` interpolation/block/helper/partial/inline-script/reviewed-asset behavior, brokered `pm.vault`, execution control, skipped requests, and mixed `pm.test` pass/fail behavior.
- `newman-sandbox-v1.iteration-data.json` is the data row used by the main corpus run.
- `newman-sandbox-v1.expected.json` records the stable PostMeter/Newman-style run outcomes asserted by automated tests.
- `newman-cancellation.collection.json` covers cancellation of pending async sandbox work.
- `differential-http-core.collection.json` is the small HTTP-core differential fixture used by `npm run postman:parity:diff`. It covers request header mutation, response assertions, `pm.info`, environment mutation, collection-variable access, and `pm.sendRequest` callback behavior against a local brokered HTTP server.
- `protocol-script-hooks.collection.json` covers imported GraphQL Before query/After response scripts and gRPC Before invoke/On message/After response scripts, including package imports, protocol request mutation, metadata/messages, response metadata/trailers/messages, `pm.message`, and protocol-specific `pm.info.eventName` values.
- `local-mock-scripts.collection.json` covers imported local mock scripts, method/path-only matching, path variables, saved example responses, top-level mock-script `await`/`return`, `pm.mock.matchRequest`, `pm.mock.sendExample`, and persistent transaction-safe `pm.state` behavior.
- `real-world-import-corpus.collection.json` is a broad representative import fixture for production-claim verification. It combines auth-heavy scripts, reviewed package references, dynamic variables, cookie metadata, visualizer assets, vault metadata, GraphQL hooks, gRPC hooks, local mock scripts, `runRequest` workflows, certificates, and file/binary body references without importing third-party copyrighted collection content.
- `desktop-observations/` contains the template path for APIs that Newman does not support and that need current Postman Desktop observations before parity rows can be marked complete. `desktop-observations/websocket-script-support-audit.json` records the current official-docs finding that WebSocket and Socket.IO requests do not expose saved/importable script hooks.

When maintainers provide canonical Newman JSON exports for the broader corpus fixtures, generate them with `newman@6.2.2`, add them beside the current expected file, and keep the automated assertions aligned with the implemented safe sandbox subset. The differential harness can also generate live Newman output with `npm run postman:parity:diff -- --newman --download-newman`. `pm.vault` is treated as a Postman sandbox parity fixture because current Newman/Postman CLI runs do not expose vault access.
